thoughts.label: SAFE MODE — MATRIX ~27h STALE (3:20 PM ENGINE MISS AGAIN)
thoughts.text: The latest signal_matrix.json is still the 2026-06-30 12:21 PDT run — ~27h old at this 3:40 PM session, far beyond the §2.5 3-hour limit. The 3:20 PM close+AH Signal Engine run did not fire (again), and no fresh run has landed since yesterday. CLU is in SAFE MODE: no new entries, existing positions managed only. Engine-schedule reliability is now the binding constraint on the book — no signal-based action has been possible across the last several sessions.

thoughts.label: SEMI SELLOFF ERASED TUESDAY'S POP — INTC -9%, SOXX -6.5%
thoughts.text: Today reversed the quarter-end rally hard in semis: INTC -9.0% (139.63->127.02, giving back the +8.8% gain to ~flat vs entry), SOXX -6.5%, MU -10.5% (1154->1033), NVDA -1.25%, QQQ -1.53%. Broad ETFs held up (VOO -0.2%). Account -2.45% on the day to $149.22 (AH basis). AH prints stabilized slightly (INTC 127.02->128.25). No thesis broke — this reads as a semi-sector pullback after a strong run, not a single-name event.

thoughts.label: RECONCILED 7/7 — NO STOP BREACHES
thoughts.text: Broker matches memory exactly: 3 LAB (INTC, ARKK, NVDA) + 3 CORE (VOO, QQQ, SOXX) + 1 DRIP (JEPQ). LAB manual stop/gap check CLEAR: INTC +14.4%, ARKK +11.2%, NVDA +4.0% above their ATR stops. NVDA is the closest to its (tight, high-failure-risk) $190.04 stop — watch on any gap-down. CORE/DRIP carry no stops (§13/§15.4). Cash $0.37, no new deposit.

thoughts.label: MU FELL INTO ITS ENTRY ZONE — BUT DEFERRED
thoughts.text: MU dropped -10.5% to $1,033, at/below its $1,050-1,100 target zone for the first time (was $1,164 yesterday). Gate is 3/5 on the stale matrix (MACD Bearish fails). Even so, no entry is on the table: SAFE MODE forbids new positions, LAB is over its 20% staging cap, and cash is $0.37. Logged as the sharpened rank-2 watch; re-evaluate on a fresh matrix once LAB dilutes <20%.

thoughts.label: DRIP STILL THE ROUTING TARGET — NO CASH TO DEPLOY
thoughts.text: Allocation LAB 25.8% / CORE 50.2% / DRIP 23.7% / cash 0.2% vs Accumulation targets 20/50/30. DRIP remains the most-underweight tier (-6.3pp) and the next-deposit target (tiebreak DRIP>CORE>LAB). With $0.37 cash there is nothing to route; wait for the next deposit. Covered-call JEPI/JEPQ can seed on yield; SCHD/DGRO/VIG/VYM stay gated on the streak/payout data fix.

thoughts.label: LAB OVER STAGING CAP — NO SINGLE-STOCK ADD AUTHORIZED
thoughts.text: LAB is 25.8% vs its 20% Accumulation cap. NET holds the cleanest gate (4/5 on the stale matrix) and MU just reached its zone, but §17.3 forbids force-selling to rebalance — the sleeve dilutes only via base growth. Combined with SAFE MODE and ~$0 cash, no LAB entry is possible.

learned_patterns.pattern: The Signal Engine has now missed multiple consecutive scheduled runs — the 3:20 PM close+AH run did not fire again, and no fresh matrix has landed since the 06-30 12:21 PDT run (~27h stale at this session). SAFE MODE has recurred across every session since, so no signal-based action has been possible for over a day. The §2.5 guard works, but the workflow cron/runner is the binding constraint on the book right now, not signal quality.

A single semi-sector pullback (INTC -9.0%, SOXX -6.5%, MU -10.5%, NVDA -1.25%) drove the whole account -2.45% in a day, while broad ETFs barely moved (VOO -0.2%). Semis are ~33% of the book (SOXX+INTC+NVDA), so semi beta dominates daily P&L. SOXX diversifies single-name risk but not sector risk — the DRIP + broad-ETF legs are what damp these swings. Building DRIP toward 30% is the concrete diversification fix.

Yesterday holdings ripped while composites stayed NEUTRAL/BEARISH; today they reversed hard. The composite (MACD-lagging) neither confirmed the pop nor flagged the drop in advance — it trails fast two-way moves in both directions. In a whippy tape the composite is a lagging confirmation, not a leading signal; the §4.1 5-gate check (Trend + MACD included) is the actual authorization, not the blended score.

The book sits at a near-target 26/50/24 LAB/CORE/DRIP mix and barely moved on a -2.5% day — the tier structure is doing its job. LAB is still 25.8% > 20% cap and dilutes only via base growth (§17.3), never trimming. DRIP -6.3pp remains the single most-underweight tier and the standing next-deposit target.

MU fell -10.5% into its $1,050-1,100 entry zone in one session, jumping from 'do-not-chase, above zone' to 'at/below zone' overnight. Entry zones get hit on gap moves, not gradual drift — but a stale matrix means the gate can't be re-scored to authorize the entry. Fresh-matrix availability, not the price level, is the gating factor on zone-triggered entries.

Dividend yields render sanely (JEPQ 9.95%, JEPI 8.10%, SCHD 3.30%) but dividend_growth_streak=0 and payout_ratio=null persist for every DRIP name, so the §15.3 screen (streak>=5, payout<80%) stays unverifiable. Covered-call names (JEPI/JEPQ, payout N/A by rule) can seed on yield alone; dividend-growth names (SCHD/DGRO/VIG/VYM) wait on the streak/payout data fix.
